vocabulary.name: IMF SDMX 3.0 Data API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Key terms and concepts used in the IMF SDMX 3.0 Data API

terms.definition: Statistical Data and Metadata eXchange — an ISO standard for the exchange of statistical data and metadata. The IMF API implements SDMX 3.0.
A named reference to a dataset in the IMF SDMX system. Each dataflow has an identifier (e.g., IFS, GFS, FM) and is associated with a data structure definition.
Data Structure Definition — describes the structure of a dataflow including its dimensions, measures, and attributes and their order in the query key.
A structural concept that, together with other dimensions, uniquely identifies an observation. Dimensions form the key for querying data (e.g., FREQ, REF_AREA, INDICATOR).
A dot-separated string of dimension values used to filter data in an API query. Use '+' to specify multiple values for a dimension and '*' to wildcard. Position corresponds to DSD dimension order.
A controlled list of codes (valid values) for a given dimension. For example, CL_COUNTRY contains all valid country codes, CL_FREQ contains frequency codes.
A collection of concepts that define the meaning of dimensions and attributes. Concepts link dimensions to their semantic meaning.
Frequency dimension — specifies data collection frequency. Common values: A (Annual), Q (Quarterly), M (Monthly), W (Weekly), D (Daily).
International Financial Statistics — IMF's primary dataset covering monthly, quarterly, and annual financial and macroeconomic data for most member countries.
World Economic Outlook — biannual IMF publication and dataset with economic projections for GDP, inflation, unemployment, and current account balances.
Government Finance Statistics — comprehensive annual data on government revenue, expenditure, and balance sheet for IMF member countries.
Fiscal Monitor — IMF publication with annual fiscal data and projections for government revenue, spending, balances, and debt.
Monetary and Financial Statistics — data on monetary aggregates, interest rates, and financial institution balance sheets.
Balance of Payments — statistics on international transactions covering the current account, capital account, and financial account.
Observation Value — the numeric value of a data point at a specific combination of dimension values and time period.
The time dimension representing the reference period for an observation. Format depends on frequency: YYYY for annual, YYYY-QN for quarterly, YYYY-MM for monthly.
Reference Area — the geographic or economic area for which data is reported. Typically ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes or IMF-specific regional codes.
The organization responsible for maintaining a structural artefact. IMF uses IMF.STA (IMF Statistics Department) and IMF as agency identifiers.
Query parameter specifying which dimension varies at the observation level in the response. Default is TIME_PERIOD, which returns time-series data. Use AllDimensions for cross-sectional data.
Consumer Price Index — a common IFS indicator measuring changes in the price level of consumer goods and services.
terms.fullName: Prices, Consumer Price Index, All items, Index

terms.definition: Real GDP Growth — annual percentage change in real Gross Domestic Product, a key WEO indicator.
terms.fullName: Gross Domestic Product, Constant Prices, Percent Change
